items.title: Mageia 10 Released For This Linux Distribution Carrying On The Mandrake Legacy
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Mageia 10 ISOs are now available for this Linux distribution long ago derived from Mandriva Linux and in turn the legendary Mandrake Linux...</article>

items.title: Arch Linux's Archinstall 4.4 Adds Dank Material Shell + Niri Desktop Option
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Ahead of the July 2026 ISO refresh for Arch Linux, a new Archinstall 4.4 release has been tagged for this text-based and very convenient installer for Arch Linux...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.2-rc1 Released: "Things Look Reasonably Normal" While Landing AMDGPU HDMI 2.1 FRL, AMD ISP4 & CAS
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>As expected, Linux 7.2-rc1 was released a brief time ago to cap off the Linux 7.2 merge window. Now it's off for eight weeks or so of testing before Linux 7.2 stable is released that will in turn go on to power the likes of Fedora 45 and Ubuntu 26.10...</article>

items.title: USB4STREAM, Optimizations, Jay & Other Popular Intel Linux News From This Quarter
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>With Q2'2026 working toward a close, here is a look back at the most popular Intel Linux and open-source news for the quarter...</article>

items.title: CachyOS June 2026 OS Released With More Performance Optimizations
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>CachyOS is out today with a new feature release for this Arch Linux powered distribution that delivers stellar out-of-the-box performance...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.3 To Introduce DRM "Color Format" Property With AMD GPU Driver Support
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>While the Linux 7.2 kernel merge window is only ending later today to cap off the feature work on this next version of the Linux kernel, already for the Linux 7.3 kernel cycle later in the year there is one notable feature on the way: the DRM color format property is being introduced and being first supported by the AMDGPU kernel graphics driver...</article>

items.title: Linux Dropping Old Drivers, AI Detected Vulnerabilities & Other Kernel Highlights Of Q2
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>With Q2'2026 quickly coming to an end, here's a recap of the most popular Linux kernel news over the past three months on Phoronix...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.2 Surpasses More Than 43 Million Lines In The Kernel Tree
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Today marks the last day of the Linux 7.2 merge window with Linux 7.2-rc1 due out later today. With the many new features and improvements merged over the past week since the Linux 7.1 stable debut, the Linux kernel source tree now exceeds 43 million lines...</article>

items.title: Vim Patches Yielding Faster GTK3 Wayland Performance: "Major Milestone"
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>For those using Vim with its GTK3 toolkit interface on Wayland, it soon should be delivering much better performance with pending patches...</article>

items.title: Nourish: A New Wayland Compositor Powered By Vulkan With Infinite Scrolling/Panning
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The newest Wayland compositor on the block is Nourish, it's a Vulkan-powered and its unique selling point is offering "infinite" zooming and panning to in effect provide an infinite workspace...</article>

items.title: Reserved THP Feature Proposed For Linux To Combine The Best Of HugeTLB & THP
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Linux kernel developer and Bytedance engineer Qi Zheng sent out a request for comments (RFC) patch series on a new feature called Reserved THP to combine the best of HugeTLB and THP kernel functionality...</article>

items.title: Linux MD RAID5 Seeing Scalability Improvements Up To 17%
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Posted to the Linux kernel mailing list this week was a new patch series working on scalability enhancements to the MD RAID5 software RAID code. Up to a 10~17% improvement was observed in some configurations with these RAID5 scalability patches...</article>

items.title: GNOME AI Assistant Adds Image Generation Support
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>In development over the past three years has been Newelle as a GNOME-aligned AI virtual assistant. Out this week is Newelle 1.4.5 and it now adds AI image generation support and a redesigned chat interface...</article>

items.title: KDE Plasma 6.7.2 To Fix KWin's Most Common Crash, Plasma 6.8 To Not Crash When Ejecting CDs
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>While Plasma 6.7.1 was just released this week following the recent stable debut of Plasma 6.7, there are already a number of fixes piling up for Plasma 6.7.2 due out in July. Plus more feature work and fixes for Plasma 6.8 as the next desktop version going Wayland-only...</article>

items.title: COSMIC's New System Monitor Is Looking Very Slick
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Not only is GNOME 51 working to replace GNOME System Monitor with its new replacement,  but over in System76's COSMIC space they have been baking their own new system monitor too...</article>

items.title: Shotcut 26.6 Released With HDR Improvements, Vulkan Display On Linux
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Shotcut 26.6 is out today as the newest major feature release for this popular cross-platform, open-source video editing solution...</article>

items.title: New Intel Linux Driver Patches Enable HDR Over DP MST Connections
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>One of the limitations of the Intel Linux driver's high dynamic range (HDR) display support is that it currently doesn't work for any DisplayPort Multi-Stream Transport "DP MST" connections, such as for daisy chaining monitors or multi-monitor docking stations. But the good news is patches are being worked on to address this Intel Linux kernel display driver shortcoming...</article>

items.title: How NVIDIA GB10 CPU Performance Compares To Vera
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Since delivering NVIDIA Vera CPU benchmarks one month ago and follow-ups like how the ARM Linux server performance has evolved in 8 years or how Vera compares to Ampere Altra that is still quite common in the marketplace, another frequent discussion point and inquiry is about the performance of NVIDIA Vera relative to NVIDIA's GB10 chip. For those curious about the per-core performance and the like, here are some benchmarks of the GB10 up against those initial Vera results.</article>

items.title: More AMD Zen 6 Prepping, Many ASUS / Lenovo / HP Laptop Improvements For Linux 7.2
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The many x86 platform driver changes have now been merged for the in-development Linux 7.2 kernel. As usual it's mostly made up of driver enhancements for benefiting modern AMD Ryzen (AI) and Intel Core (Ultra) laptops...</article>

items.title: Experimental Code Enables Per-Monitor Backgrounds For GNOME Shell
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>One of the limitations of GNOME's current multi-monitor handling is that the same background is used across the displays. For those that want to enjoy per-monitor background selection, some experimental / proof-of-concept code is now working to allow such per-monitor backgrounds to work with the modern GNOME desktop...</article>

items.title: Intel ANV Vulkan Driver Now Enables Descriptor Heaps By Default
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Back in early May was the experimental enabling of Vulkan descriptor heaps for the Intel ANV open-source driver. After nearly two months of continued testing and improvements, the VK_EXT_descriptor_heap support for Intel graphics on Linux is now enabled by default...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.2 Fixes Where PCIe Devices Could Be Inadvertently Restricted To 2.5 GT/s
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The PCI/PCIe subsystem changes have been merged this week as we approach the end of the Linux 7.2 merge window...</article>

items.title: Intel ISPC 1.31 Brings New Targets For Nova Lake, Experimental PowerPC 64-bit
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Intel engineers on Thursday released the newest version of the Intel Implicit SPMD Program Compiler, ISPC. The ISPC 1.31 supports their variant of the C programming language with extensions for Single Program, Multiple Data programming for leveraging their range of CPU and GPU hardware...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.2 Adds New Driver For Wacom W9000 Pen-Enabled Touchscreens
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The input subsystem changes were merged this week for Linux 7.2, which is seeing its merge window wrap up on Sunday. Most notable with the input updates is the introduction of the "wacom_w9000" for supporting newer, pen-enabled touchscreens...</article>

items.title: Coreboot 26.06 Brings Support For Intel Nova Lake, AMD Strix Halo & 31 New Boards
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Coreboot 26.06 is out today as the latest quarterly feature release for this software project providing open-source system firmware support for a growing number of platforms...</article>

items.title: Ubuntu 26.10 Snapshot 2 Released For Monthly Testing
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Daily ISOs of Ubuntu 26.10 "Stonking Stingray" continue to be published, but for those preferring something a bit more regulated, out today is Ubuntu 26.10 Snapshot 2 as the second monthly ISO image...</article>

items.title: Linux Foundation & Others Launch "Akrites" To Defend Open-Source Software From AI-Enabled Exploits
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The Linux Foundation along with others like Amazon, Anthropic, OpenAI, NVIDIA, Microsoft, Red Hat, and others have joined forces to launch Akrites. The Akrites project is aiming to help defend critical open-source software from the brisk pace of new AI/LLM-discovered software bugs and vulnerabilities in ensuring that said issues are effectively addressed before they can be exploited by bad actors...</article>

items.title: DXVK 3.0 Released With DXBC-SPIRV For Shader Compilation, Descriptor Heaps By Default
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Philip Rebohle announced the release today of DXVK 3.0 as the latest major feature release for this Direct3D 8 / 9 / 10 / 11 implementation atop the Vulkan API for use by Wine and Valve's Steam Play (Proton)...</article>

items.title: Academy Software Foundation Announces The "Wayland For Artists Working Group"
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>The Academy Software Foundation that advances open-source efforts for the VFX/cinema industry and more with the likes of OpenVDB, OpenMoonRay, Open Shading Language, and other projects, has announced the formation of a new working group to help with Wayland adoption for artists...</article>

items.title: Updated Raspberry Pi OS With Linux 6.18 LTS Delivers Some Performance Benefits
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Last week marked the release of an updated Raspberry Pi OS that moved to Linux 6.18 LTS from its former Linux 6.12 kernel base along with making a number of other package updates. Given the jump to the newer Long Term Support kernel and other improvements, I ran some fresh benchmarks on the Raspberry Pi 5 (Raspberry Pi 500+) to see the performance difference out of the updated operating system.</article>

items.title: Servo 0.3 Released With The Demo Browser Becoming More Useful
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Servo 0.3 released today as the latest version of this modern browser engine developed in Rust. With Servo 0.3 the demo servoshell browser is becoming more useful and supporting additional modern web features while Servo also continues to possess much potential moving forward on the embedded front as an alternative to the likes of the Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF)...</article>

items.title: Linux 7.2 Drops Ancient PROFIBUS Driver: Ported From SCO Unix In 1998, Unused For Years
items.author: Michael Larabel
items.content: <article>Linux 7.2 is continuing the trend of removing obsolete hardware drivers for which the code hasn't seen any maintenance in years and there are no believed users left of said drivers, especially those that would be running modern mainline versions of the Linux kernel. The char/misc changes merged dropped two more obsolete drivers from the Linux source tree...</article>
